name: Teddy Powell
abstract: Teddy Powell (born Teodoro Paolella; March 1, 1905 – November 17, 1993) was an American jazz musician, band leader, composer, and arranger. Some of his compositions were written under the pseudonym Freddy James.

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: Well I was with the band — Teddy Powell had a band. ... And I joined the band. I remember being with the band about three or four months
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: You also played with a big band, speaking of big bands, lead by Teddy Powell. ... I took Charlie Ventura’s place.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: I had just left Teddy Powell’s band... Teddy Powell’s band I think... I’m with Powell’s band
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: my mother’s brothers were on the road — a brother was on the road — with a lot of different bands, Clyde McCoy, Buddy Rodgers, Raymond Scott, Teddy Powell
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 23, 2003 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: I went to see my brother who was working the Hotel Sherman in Chicago with a very good band, the leader you won’t know, a guy named Teddy Powell.

about_quotes.speaker: Buster Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I did some arranging for Powell -- the last name; he had a big band down there in the Famous Door. I did some arranging for him.
